title: The Emerging Developer Playbook
intro: How to actually use this terminal and these programs, sequenced for a developer building from solo deals toward institutional scale. This is strategy distilled from how the programs really interact — not legal or investment advice.

edge.title: Where your edge actually is
points: You are not going to out-bid institutions on marketed deals. Your edge is OFF-MARKET: underutilized parcels the engine surfaces before they list, owners you contact directly (names and mailing addresses are in the data), and city land nobody without a plan can win.

The engine's walk-away price is your discipline. The spread between estimated land cost and walk-away is your negotiating room — deals die from overpaying for land, almost never from anything else you control.

Small is a superpower, not a limitation. The 6–10 unit 485-x tier (10-year FULL tax exemption, NO affordability requirement, outside Manhattan) is the single most generous per-dollar program in NYC and it is designed for exactly your size. Institutions can't be bothered; you can.

Tax exemptions ARE the profit margin. Run the numbers in the deal board: almost nothing pencils with full taxes, almost everything decent pencils abated. Your real business is converting entitlements + exemptions into yield.

stages.play: Buy a small underbuilt lot in the Bronx/Brooklyn/Queens (the engine's SOLO + market tier), build 6-10 units, take the 485-x small-building exemption (no affordability requirement), rent at market or Section 8 VPS.
stages.programs: 485-x small tier · Section 8 VPS rents · conventional 85% LTC construction loan (your Ponce/Northeast thesis) · CPC if banks hesitate
stages.money: Equity $250-700k per deal. Buy BELOW the walk-away price or don't buy. Section 8 VPS in the Bronx often beats market rent — that's yield insurance, not charity.
stages.why: Track record is the currency of this industry. Three completed small buildings = you exist to lenders, HPD, and LPs.

stages.play: Same thesis, bigger lots: 485-x standard (20% affordable @ 80% AMI — cheap for what you get back in a 35-year exemption), stack the City of Yes UAP +20% FAR bonus, keep Section 8 on market units where VPS is favorable. Start bidding HPD RFPs on the CITY-tagged parcels.
stages.programs: 485-x <100u tier · UAP bonus FAR · Section 8 · NYC Acquisition Fund for site control · M/WBE certification if eligible (real scoring preference on city dispositions) · HPD RFPs
stages.money: Equity $700k-3M — this is where the MID bucket and your first JV/LP capital come in. The Acquisition Fund can carry site control so your equity goes to construction.
stages.why: City land at nominal cost + a 35-year exemption + bonus FAR is the best risk-adjusted deal in the region — but HPD picks credible teams, which is what Stage 1 bought you.

stages.play: All-affordable ELLA/Mix & Match projects where your profit is the developer fee (~10% of TDC — fee on a $30M project is $3M), plus suburban plays: IDA PILOT deals in Westchester, 8-30g set-asides in wealthy CT towns, 40A:20 PILOT deals in NJ redevelopment areas.
stages.programs: ELLA / Mix & Match / 4% LIHTC + bonds · SARA if senior · HCR Unified Funding · IDA PILOTs · CT 8-30g · NJ Aspire + PILOTs · FHLBNY AHP grants
stages.money: Little-to-no equity — the stack funds it; the game is winning competitive awards and managing complexity. Partner with an experienced affordable co-developer on your first one: you trade some fee for the track record that unlocks every future award.
stages.why: Fee-driven deals decouple your growth from your bank account. This is how small shops become institutions.

screen.title: What to look for (and avoid) — deal screening habits
look: Land basis vs walk-away: the engine's #1 signal. Big spread = real deal or mispriced data — verify either way.
6-10 buildable units outside Manhattan → automatic 485-x small-tier candidate. The sweet spot.
CITY tag = HPD/EDC disposition watchlist. Track the RFP pipeline; these are won with plans, not bids.
Adjacent underbuilt lots (the engine often surfaces them in rows — St Marks, Gerry St) → assemblage = bigger FAR utilization on one loan.
Section 8-strong areas (Bronx, parts of Queens): VPS above market = higher NOI at LOWER vacancy risk.
In CT: any site in a town under the 10% affordable threshold (Greenwich, Westport, Ridgefield, New Canaan, Darien) where you can OPTION cheaply — 8-30g flips the zoning power dynamic.
In NJ: 'Area in Need of Redevelopment' designations near the GWB corridor — designation + PILOT + Aspire is the whole capital stack.
In Westchester: Pro-Housing designated municipalities — the state is literally telling you where approvals and money flow.
avoid: Anything where only the ELLA path pencils AND you have no affordable track record yet — subsidy awards are competitive; don't bet your equity on winning one.
Suspiciously cheap suburban land (the engine floors at $30k/acre for this reason) — wetlands, slopes, no sewer. If it looks free, it's unbuildable.
Flood zones, landmarked buildings, irregular slivers — the engine filters most, but verify on anything you pursue.
Overpaying because the pro-forma 'still works' — the walk-away number exists so future-you doesn't hate present-you.
100+ unit projects before you're ready: 485-x wage floors ($35+/hr, +12% hard cost modeled) change the math and the operational demands.

moves.title: Force multipliers most emerging developers skip

items.d: If eligible, certify (city AND state). It carries real scoring weight on HPD/EDC dispositions and unlocks Acquisition Fund terms up to ~95% of cost. Takes weeks; pays for years.
Control sites with 6-12 month options while you file for the exemption/variance/8-30g. Spend thousands controlling what you'd otherwise spend millions buying — entitle first, close second.
The engine gives you owner names + mailing addresses on every target. A steady 50-letters-a-month habit to underbuilt-lot owners is how off-market deals actually happen. One yes a year is a business.
Architect feasibility + zoning analysis BEFORE closing (the engine's numbers are screens). A $15k feasibility study that kills a bad deal is the best ROI in real estate.

Ponce, Northeast, CPC, and community banks that know your zip codes will out-lend money-center banks on your deals. Bring them the engine's underwriting format — DSCR-sized, tax-adjusted — and you'll look institutional from deal one.
ELLA/Mix & Match rounds, HCR Unified Funding (annual), CHFA/DOH rounds, FHLBNY AHP — these run on cycles. Stage 3 is won by teams whose applications are ready BEFORE the round opens.

sequence.title: A realistic first 24 months
steps: Months 1-3: Pick 2-3 target neighborhoods from the deal board. Start the letter campaign on top SOLO targets. Get M/WBE certified if eligible. Meet Ponce/Northeast/CPC with a sample underwriting package.
Months 3-9: Option or contract the first site below walk-away. Feasibility study, then file plans + 485-x. Close with the construction loan.
Months 9-18: Build #1. Keep the letter machine running — the pipeline is the business. Track HPD RFPs on CITY-tagged lots and bid one with a strong plan (partner if needed).
Months 18-24: Lease up #1 (Section 8 + market), refi or hold per the numbers, roll equity into deal #2-3. Start conversations with an affordable co-developer about a Stage-3 fee deal.
Throughout: never buy above walk-away, never skip feasibility, never let the pipeline go quiet.

disclaimer: Strategy education based on current program rules and market math — not legal, tax, or investment advice. Verify every program's current terms (sources in the Program Library) and use professionals for deals.
